reasoning: The base value of 1.266 for May 2025 is low relative to the 6-month average (~1.5) and 12-month average (~1.8). The Cleveland Fed's real interest rate model is highly volatile month-to-month, meaning a mean-reverting bounce is probable in the short term, giving a higher chance of an increase for June. However, over the longer term, the broader macroeconomic trend of falling nominal rates (driven by anticipated Fed rate cuts through late 2025) will exert downward pressure on the 1-year real rate. This structural downward trend gradually offsets the short-term mean reversion, moving the probabilities closer to 0.5 for the later resolution dates.
